name: Easter Bread

ingredients.item: all-purpose flour, with a bit of extra for the
stick softened butter, cut into cubes

method: Using nonstick cooking spray, coat a large mixing bowl.
Combine milk and 1 teaspoon sugar in a small dish or liquid
measuring cup, then sprinkle yeast on top. Allow settling for 8
minutes or until foamy.
Combine flour, remaining 12 cup sugar, and salt in the bowl of a
stand mixer fitted with the dough hook. Combine the milk,
yeast, and eggs in a mixing bowl. Mix for about 5 minutes, or
until a very soft dough develops. Increase the mixer speed to
medium-high and gradually add the butter, a tablespoon at a
time, mixing thoroughly after each addition, until the dough
pulls away from the sides of the bowl, approximately 15
minutes.
Place dough in greased basin, cover, and set aside to rise for 1 to
1 1/2 hours in a warm place.
Meanwhile, create hard-boiled eggs by placing eggs in a
medium pot with enough water to cover them. Bring to a boil
over medium heat. Cover and remove from heat. Allow for 11
minutes of resting time before removing from the pan and
plunging into the icy water.
Pour 1 cup boiling water into two separate dishes. To each bowl,
add 1 teaspoon vinegar and the appropriate food coloring. One
egg at a time, add to the pan and set aside for 5 to 10 minutes,
depending on the desired color. Remove with a slotted spoon
from the bowl and dry on a wire rack placed over a baking
sheet.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and prepare a large
baking sheet with parchment paper. Divide the dough into three
equal pieces on a lightly floured surface. Make a 16" long rope
out of each component. Place the ropes lengthwise on the
baking sheet that has been prepared. Pinch the top ends of the
strings together, then braid them tightly together. Make a circle
with the limitations and squeeze them together.
Press colored eggs into the braid, then cover and let rise for 30
minutes or until the dough has doubled in size.
Finish with an egg wash and sprinkles—Bake for 30 minutes, or
until brown.
